session_hint: the TLS handshake failure investigation on mobile clients
question: What was the root cause of the TLS handshake failures on mobile clients?
turns: user
About 15% of mobile users reporting SSL connection errors on login. Desktop, curl, and Postman all succeed. Started after cert renewal last month.
user
First theory: cipher suite mismatch. Server supports all modern suites including ECDHE-RSA-AES256-GCM-SHA384. TLS 1.2 and 1.3 both enabled. No reason mobile should reject this.
user
Tried disabling OCSP stapling on nginx and reloading. Failure rate unchanged on mobile. OCSP not the issue, ruled out.
user
Checked SNI configuration — server returns the correct leaf certificate for each hostname when tested with openssl s_client -servername. SNI is working, ruled that out.
user
Captured a Wireshark trace from a failing Android device. Handshake aborts with a TLS alert after the server sends its certificate chain. Client rejects immediately.
user
Desktop browsers and modern Android (8+) succeed. Failing clients are all Android 6 and 7. These versions use an older system trust store.
user
Ran openssl verify on our certificate chain from a simulated Android 6 trust store perspective. Got: 'unable to get local issuer certificate'. Chain validation fails.
user

Found the actual bug: when the cert was renewed last month, the engineer updated only the leaf certificate file in /etc/nginx/ssl/cert.pem. The intermediate certificate file was not updated and still references the old intermediate. nginx is serving an incomplete chain — leaf cert only.
user

Desktop clients and Android 8+ silently fetch the missing intermediate via the Authority Information Access (AIA) extension. Android 6/7 does not follow AIA and requires the server to send the complete chain.
user
Root cause: the intermediate certificate was missing from the nginx TLS bundle after the renewal. Old Android clients that do not support AIA fetching fail chain validation.
user
Fix: concatenate the renewed leaf cert and the correct intermediate into /etc/nginx/ssl/cert.pem, reload nginx. Mobile TLS errors drop to zero immediately.
